habitat: Mountainous terrain of the Tibetan plateau; cliffs; rocky ledges; at 600–4500 m
story: The Himalayan Vulture (Gyps himalayensis) is an extraordinary bird that belongs to the Accipitridae family. It is most commonly observed in Mountainous terrain of the Tibetan plateau; cliffs; rocky ledges; at 600–4500 m, where its behaviors contribute to the ecological dynamics of the area. Listed as Near Threatened (NT) by the IUCN, this species' future depends on continued conservation action. The story of the Himalayan Vulture is one of adaptation and resilience, offering valuable lessons for conservationists. This poster aims to bring the Himalayan Vulture into focus, highlighting its beauty and the importance of its habitat.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
